text: The separation of ethylene (C2H4) from mixtures containing smaller amounts of acetylene (C2H2) and ethane (C2H6) is a major industrial problem in the production of polymer-grade feedstocks. Here, a stable metal–organic framework (MOF), HIAM-111, that favours the adsorption of light hydrocarbons is synthesized and characterized. Through adsorption isotherms and thermodynamic analyses, the highly selective adsorption of C2H2 and C2H6 towards C2H4 is validated, and the direct separation of ethylene with high purity in a single step is confirmed. By implementing a Dual-site Langmuir-Freundlich model in the investigation of competitive adsorption at industrially relevant conditions, the performance of HIAM-111 for a C2H2/C2H4/C2H6 (1/90/9) ternary mixture at 298 K showed a purity of 99.5% ethylene at a high ethylene productivity of 0.0248 L/kg. The stability of the MOF structure towards various solvents, as well as its adsorption performance for multiple cycles are confirmed. Elaborated on the basis of experimental data as well as molecular simulations, the performance of HIAM-111 confirms that it is a high-efficient energy-saving material for olefin purification.
